commit.message:
Fix GROUP/NORMAL nodeType preference in BFS lasso/AIC checks

Java's expandTables rewrites GROUP parent pointers to NORMAL when both
reach the same cell at the same BFS distance (nodeType 0 < 1). This
affects which chains are detected as lassos and which GROUP_NODE virtual
entries appear in the chain.

Instead of rewriting parent pointers globally (which regresses other
puzzles), store NORMAL alternates in altRetOn/altRetOff maps during BFS
snap expansion. Then in _buildAndCheckLasso and _aicIsValid, build the
'Java chain' by preferring alternate NORMAL parents and only inserting
GROUP_NODE virtual entries when no alternate exists.

Extract _lassoCheck as a shared static method used by both nice loop
and AIC lasso detection paths.

Fixes puzzle .6....5.17...516..9....2.7...1..6...25..4..6...9...7.......829...7.2..4...2.1....
(expected 2560, was 2530)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Restructure: purpose-built agents with proper trigger routing

New agents:
  deployment-validator — triggered by Release pipeline (BuildSucceeded)
    Proactively checks health after every deploy, identifies which
    microservice was deployed, picks the right diagnostic skill.
  vm-ops-agent — triggered by Azure Monitor alerts (title contains 'disk')
    Handles VM infrastructure issues using disk-pressure-diagnosis skill.

Release triggers:
  Post-Deploy Validation → Release pipeline succeeds → deployment-validator
  Build Failure Investigation → Build pipeline fails → incident-handler

Incident filters:
  vm-disk-alert → title contains 'disk' → vm-ops-agent
  auto-investigate → all other Azure Monitor alerts → incident-handler

Release pipeline updated with 'services' parameter so agent can
read which microservices were deployed via GetPipelineRunVariables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add two tests to cover the error-propagation paths in installer::pipeline::install()
that were previously never exercised:

- install_with_invalid_manifest_toml_returns_manifest_error: passes a syntactically
  invalid TOML file as the manifest; triggers the map_err/.? branch on the
  manifest::parse_and_validate() call (line 81).

- install_with_malformed_lockfile_returns_manifest_error: writes invalid TOML to an
  existing lockfile path; triggers the lockfile::read() error branch wrapped in

README previously had no enumeration of the rules GauntletCI runs,
making it impossible for users to discover what analysis they are
getting. The example output referenced specific GCI IDs with no
reference for what they mean.

- Table lists all 36 rules (GCI0001-GCI0037, GCI0028 reserved)
- Each entry includes ID, name, and one-line detection description
- Note clarifies GCI0028 is intentionally unassigned

Fixes: audit finding 'align README with reality'

commit.message:
[CORPUS] Manual PR ingestion: hydrator, normalizer, CLI command (#53)

- GitHubRestHydrator (IPullRequestHydrator): fetches PR details, files, review
  comments, commits, and diff concurrently via GitHub REST API; saves raw
  snapshots (pr.json, files.json, review-comments.json) to fixture raw/ dir;
  maps responses to domain models; uses GITHUB_TOKEN env var for auth
- GitHubApiModels: internal DTOs for GitHub REST API response deserialization
- ManualSeedProvider (IDiscoveryProvider): converts static PR URL list to
  PullRequestCandidates; GetProviderName=manual, no incremental sync
- FixtureNormalizer: deterministic HydratedPullRequest -> FixtureMetadata;
  infers tags (async, contract-change, early-return, null-safety, state-mutation,
  logging, exception-flow, api-change) from diff content; detects dominant
  language from changed files; defaults tier to Discovery
- CorpusCommand + add-pr subcommand: 'gauntletci corpus add-pr --url <url>'
  hydrates a PR, normalizes it, saves metadata.json + SQLite index entry, and
  prints fixture ID and tags; --db and --fixtures flags for path overrides
- GauntletCI.Cli now references GauntletCI.Corpus
- corpus subcommand registered in Program.cs

commit.message:
refactor(mcp): centralize constants, add error sanitization, decompose mcp-server (#3705)

* refactor(mcp): centralize constants, add error sanitization, decompose mcp-server

Address six review findings from the qsv-agent-skills code review:

- Centralize duplicate magic numbers (50MB output, 1MB stderr, 5s timeout)
  into tool-constants.ts; update all consumers
- Add sanitizeErrorForClient() to strip absolute paths from MCP error
  responses; apply at 5 errorResult() call sites in tool-handlers.ts
- Guard against ReDoS in handleSearchToolsCall by rejecting regex
  patterns exceeding 200 chars
- Extract WorkingDirManager class (316 lines) from mcp-server.ts,
  reducing it from 1616 to 1308 lines (-19%)
- Add 44 unit tests for file-operations.ts (path resolution, auto-index,
  temp files, param aliases, conversion args)
- Add 26 unit tests for command-guidance.ts (guidance map, parameter
  enhancement, description enrichment with emoji conventions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update .claude/skills/src/tool-handlers.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(mcp): clear manual flag on "auto" sync, address Copilot review findings

Fix bug where passing "auto" to qsv_set_working_dir after a manual set
silently skipped the roots sync while reporting success. Also remove
orphaned JSDoc in parquet-bridge, document sanitizer space-path limitation,
and clarify ReDoS guard scope in tool-handlers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mcp): restore manual flag on failed "auto" sync attempt

If syncWorkingDirFromRoots() throws after clearing the manual flag,
restore it via markManuallySet() so roots notifications don't silently
change the directory — matching the error message that tells the user
the directory remains unchanged.

* fix: use withInMemoryScrolling instead of withRouterConfig for scroll options

withRouterConfig() accepts RouterConfigOptions which does not include
scrollPositionRestoration/anchorScrolling/scrollOffset — those belong
in withInMemoryScrolling() (InMemoryScrollingOptions). Replace
withRouterConfig with withInMemoryScrolling to fix TS2353 compile error.

* fix: apply review feedback — scrollOffset, queryParamMap.get for URL guard

- app.config.ts: add scrollOffset: [0, 72] to withInMemoryScrolling so
  the 72px header offset comment matches actual behaviour
- compiler.component.ts: use queryParamMap.get('url') instead of
  queryParams['url'] at both navigate guards — queryParams['url'] can be
  string|string[], making the equality check unreliable when query params
  are repeated; queryParamMap.get() always returns string|null

scrollOffset is a RouterConfigOptions property, not InMemoryScrollingOptions.
Moving it to withRouterConfig({ scrollOffset: [0, 72] }) fixes the
TS2353 build error introduced when applying the reviewer's suggestion.

scrollOffset is not a property of InMemoryScrollingOptions or RouterConfigOptions
in Angular 21. The correct API is ViewportScroller.setOffset([0, 72]), called inside
provideAppInitializer before any await so the injection context is still active.

- Remove withRouterConfig (not needed)
- Add ViewportScroller to @angular/common import
- Call inject(ViewportScroller).setOffset([0, 72]) in provideAppInitializer
- Update comment to describe actual behavior

commit.message:
Renderer: enforce 2-tile scroll margin at visible viewport edges

The player could walk to the very edge of the CSS-visible window and
continue scrolling with no surrounding context. Add SCROLL_MARGIN = 2:
the viewport now shifts to keep the player at least 2 tiles inset from
each visible edge, so surrounding walls/tiles are always shown.
When the map boundary prevents centring, the margin relaxes gracefully
(void tiles appear on the opposite side rather than losing the player).

checked: leo.tsai

Fix mobile: keep player visible when viewport clamps at map edge

Cover-mode CSS scaling clips the canvas on one axis. On portrait mobile
the canvas overflows horizontally, so only the centre ~29 of 60 columns
are visible. When the player walked to a map edge, the map-bounds clamp
(vpX = 0) pushed the player into the hidden left portion of the canvas.

- Renderer: track visibleCols/visibleRows (set via setVisibleViewport)
- Renderer.draw: two-step viewport calc — clamp to map bounds first,
  then shift to guarantee player stays within the visible CSS window
  (may render void tiles at the far edge rather than losing the player)
- Game: expose setRendererViewport() to forward values to the renderer
- main.ts: fitCanvas now returns visibleCols/visibleRows (computed from
  availW/availH divided by tile size × scale) and wires them to game

feat: add scrollbar and async page rendering for PDF viewer

Scrollbar:
- Custom scrollbar track on the right edge with draggable thumb
- Click on track jumps to position, drag thumb for fine control
- Delta-based drag tracking so thumb stays exactly under mouse
- Auto-hides when content fits in viewport

Async rendering:
- Pages render on background threads (no UI freeze)
- Placeholder shown while pages load
- Arc<Vec<u8>> for PDF bytes to avoid cloning
- Pending render tracking to prevent duplicate work

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: load standard MM3 parameters from .fld files

Add include_standard parameter to _mm3_import_ff, load_mm3_fld,
and ForceField.from_mm3_fld. When enabled, parses the full MM3
parameter body (rows 60-1800) in addition to the substructure
section, matching how MacroModel resolves parameters.

This fixes issue #197: the published Donoghue 2008 force field
was missing 30/38 bond and 55/78 angle parameters, causing
C-H stretches at 25,000 cm⁻¹ (should be <4,000). With standard
parameters loaded, the objective score drops from 139,911 to 9,530
(93% reduction).

The published FF still does not beat the Seminario baseline (~104)
due to MM3* functional form differences (stretch-bend coupling,
etc.) not implemented in OpenMM. Xfail gates remain in place.

Closes #197
